item.text: I’m scanning the production Rust sources under `crates/*/src` for actual `ToolContext` struct-literal construction sites, then I’ll filter out definitions/non-production paths and verify each hit with line context.
I found initial candidates in `orbit-engine` and `orbit-core`; next I’m validating each match is an actual construction expression (not a type position) and collecting unique file paths.
I’m doing one broader `ToolContext` search in `crates/*/src` to ensure we didn’t miss any odd formatting cases (like line breaks) and confirm the final file set is complete.
I’m validating each candidate file with local context so we only return true struct-literal construction sites.

item.text: I’ve confirmed direct literals, and now I’m running a safety check for assignments like `= ToolContext` that could hide brace-on-next-line constructors.
